content.assessment: This proposal is the most congruent with implementation-planning criteria of monitoring, adaptation, and failure containment, since it targets the root structural gap identified across the evidence base: the historical absence of any standing evidence or feedback infrastructure underlying repeated top-down policy swings. It is model-agnostic and thus pilotable independent of which local delivery mechanism (T1-T4) is chosen, but its central risk is that a purely informational infrastructure has no binding force and could be circumvented by the same centralized, politically-driven decision pattern that has historically overridden evidence, especially given documented curtailment and possible deprioritization of consultation/monitoring bodies post-2010.
opportunities: Could be sequenced first, ahead of any structural reform, to generate the region-specific evidence needed given the spatially uneven depopulation pattern before committing to T2/T3/T4 mechanisms (F-live-implementation_planning-04)
strengths: Directly targets the documented root cause of policy instability: absence of institutionalized feedback across nearly a century of cyclical top-down decisions (F-live-implementation_planning-02)
Model-agnostic design allows piloting the registry/threshold infrastructure independently of, and prior to, any specific consolidation or retention model, reducing sequencing risk
Cost-verification and standardized accounting elements directly respond to documented gaps in transport/cost accounting practice
threats: Data reported by tankerület (district) centers could be gamed or inconsistently reported absent independent audit capacity, undermining the entire premise of transparency

Being purely informational, the registry could become a symbolic institution similar to prior unenforced safeguards, echoing the decade-long non-enforcement pattern seen for existing legal mechanisms (F-live-implementation_planning-07)
weaknesses: No binding decision rule is proposed alongside the evidence infrastructure, so actors retain full discretion to ignore it exactly as historical evidence shows for prior swings (F-live-implementation_planning-02)

A new registry or monitoring body would need resourcing and institutional protection in a governance environment where consultation mechanisms have already been curtailed or deprioritized post-2010 (F-live-implementation_planning-10)
